title: Dependency Scan Block And Override
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: dependency scan block and override.
assumptions: Scans produce deterministic findings that can be correlated with release targets and scan profiles.
Manual overrides are authenticated, recorded, and traceable to an approver identity and organizational role.
Resource reservation (agents, scanners) is necessary to run scans and is correctly reflected in the system state.
Promotion to downstream targets requires explicit satisfaction of configured gates or documented overrides.
risks: Unauthorized or improperly audited overrides could permit vulnerable artifacts to be promoted.
Scanner false negatives or misconfiguration may result in undetected vulnerabilities progressing through gates.
Resource exhaustion (agents or scanners) may cause CI backlogs and delay critical releases.
Complex multi-step approval flows increase operational friction and may lead to ad-hoc bypass processes.
Inconsistent binding between scan profiles and release targets can produce environment-specific regressions.
Audit log tampering or lack of immutable records undermines post-incident forensics and compliance.
